column2: User-Specific Search History Isolation
column11:
Step Description:
1. Login as User A
2. Perform searches: ""laptop"", ""mouse""
3. Logout from User A
4. Login as User B
5. Perform searches: ""phone"", ""tablet""
6. Check search history dropdown for User B
7. Logout and login back as User A
8. Check search history dropdown for User A
Expected Result:
User A sees only their searches: ""laptop"", ""mouse""
User B sees only their searches: ""phone"", ""tablet""
No cross-contamination of search history between users
column13:
User A sees only their searches: "laptop", "mouse"
User B sees only their searches: "phone", "tablet"
No cross-contamination of search history between users
column15:
1. Login as User A
2. Perform searches: "laptop", "mouse"
3. Logout from User A
4. Login as User B
5. Perform searches: "phone", "tablet"
6. Check search history dropdown for User B
7. Logout and login back as User A
8. Check search history dropdown for User A

column2: Guest vs Registered User Search History
column11:

4. Logout and browse as guest again
Expected Result:
Guest search history is separate from registered user
Registered user starts with clean search history
Guest retains their previous search history (if stored locally)
Clear separation between user types

column2: Search History Clearing on User Switch

- User B's own previous search history
- No traces of User A's search history remain

column2: Search History Persistence for Registered Users

Expected Result:

""wireless mouse"" and ""mechanical keyboard"" still appear
Search history is properly restored after re-login

column2: Multiple Users Same Device Search History
column11:
Step Description:
1. Login as User A, search for ""gaming laptop""
2. Logout User A
3. Login as User B on same browser, search for ""office chair""
4. Logout User B  
5. Login as User C on same browser, search for ""smartphone""
6. Switch back to User A and check search history
Expected Result:
Each user maintains independent search history:
- User A: ""gaming laptop""
- User B: ""office chair""  
- User C: ""smartphone""
No mixing or contamination between users

Set up the firebase settings in environment.yml
column4: The settings are set up successfully
column10:
1. Open https://console.firebase.google.com/
Click Add project
Enter project name
Click Create project
When the project is created, click Continue

2. Sender settings
Click Project settings
Click Service accounts
Click Generate new private key
In the popup window click Generate key
Open the downloaded JSON file and copy all fields to the corresponding fields in the
appSettings.json
Copy private key value and put the resulting value in the Key Vault (Azure portal)

3. Receiver settings
On the Project Overview page click Add Web app
Enter application name
Click Register app
In the Add Firebase SDK section you will find settings that should be placed in the
PushMessages.FcmReceiverOptions section in the appSettings.json
Click Continue to console
Click Project settings
Click Cloud Messaging
In the Web Push certificates section click Generate key pair

3. Save settings in environment.yml and push the pull request
4. Open the Frontend application > Console

column4: The merged cart should show Product A (Qty: 3) (sum of both carts). No duplicate products should appear.

column2: Cart Merge When Store Currency Changes
